content_en:
"I Ton Hajr said, “Related by Abu Daawood (1048); An-Nassaaee (1389); and Al-Haakim, with a Hasan chain from Abu Salamah, from Jaabir #, Marfoo’an (i.e., the narration is from the Prophet 3%). And in sahih Abee Daawood, Al-Albaanee gave the hadith a ‘sahih’ grade.

Chapter - 22 The Manners Of Supplication 497

were specified, then people would rely on it and forsake supplicating at other times. With this understanding in mind, it is surprising to see people who want to know precisely when the hour is.”

e) When a fasting person breaks his fast: Abu Hurairah related that the Messenger of Allah #% said,

“Three [people] do not have their supplications rejected: the fasting person until he breaks his fast..."

f) The supplication of a person who is wronged, of the traveler, and of a father against his son: Ibn ‘Abbaas w reported that the Messenger of Allah #% said the following words to Mu’aadh Ibn Jabal 4 upon sending him to Yemen:

abl 25 ks Sf tt Vy dy

“Verily, you are going to a people from the People of the Book. When you reach them, then invite them to bear witness that none has the right to be worshipped but Allah and that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah...”

And he #% went on to say,

“And protect yourself from the supplication of the wronged person, for indeed, there is no veil between him and Allah.”” ]

And Abu Hurairah # related that the Messenger of Allah 3% said,

") Related by At-Tirmidhee, who said, “This hadith is Hasan’; by Ibn Maajah (1752), and in grading his narration, Al-Albaanee ruled that it is authentic (1432-1779).

8585) cpgllsch $325 13s GY Suen cigs bob

“Three supplications that are answered and about which there is no doubt (i.e., no doubt that they will be answered): the

supplication of the person who is wronged, the supplication of the traveler, and the supplication of the father against his son.”

The person who wrongs others should desist from his wrongdoing, for there is no veil between Allah and the people he wronged; their supplications are quickly answered. The traveler should take advantage of his time by invoking Allah 3%, for his supplications are answered. And parents should be careful not invoke Allah 3 against their children; a father’s supplication is answered, and in the heat of his anger, he may say words that he will later regret.

g) During battle and when the call to prayer is made: Sahl Ibn Sa‘ad .% reported that the Messenger of Allah #¢ said,

“Two [supplications] are not rejected or are rarely rejected: supplication at the time of the call [to prayer] and when the battle becomes intense, when they [the fighting armies] clash and fight intensely.”"1]

During times of affliction, or when one has a specific need, one may supplicate with the invocation of Dheen-Noon: Sa‘ad Ibn Abee Waqqaas # related that the Messenger of Allah 38 said,

Boe EH SY re A oy og 5h5 25 Y ob ss B55

' Related by Abu Daawood (2540), and one narration contains an addition

from Sahl Ibn Sa’ad 4 from the Prophet #: “And the time of rain.” Al-

Albaanee said, “With the exception of, ‘and the time of rain it is authentic. Ad-Daarimee related the same (1200).

Chapter - 22 The Manners Of Supplication 499

“This is the invocation of Dheen-Noon which he supplicated with when he was inside the belly of the whale: ‘None has the right to be worshipped but You (O Allah); how perfect You are! Verily, I was from the wrongdoers.’ No Muslim man supplicates with it concerning anything except that Allah answers [his supplication] for him.’"]

When it is raining: The following is related in a hadith: “Seek to supplicate when armies meet, when the Iqaamah for prayer is made, and when it is raining.”

Situations Wherein One Can Reasonably Hope For His Supplications To Be Answered

The night of ‘Arafah, for those that are there at the Mawgif (those that are performing Hajj): The Prophet 2 legislated that, on the Day of ‘Arafah, pilgrims should combine the Dhuhr and ‘Asr prayer and perform them during the time for Dhuhr, so that each pilgrim can then free himself to supplicate to Allah 3. During Hajj and on the Day of ‘Arafah, when the Prophet #% finished performing the two prayers, he went to Al-Mawgif, beneath the mountain. There he stopped, remained on his mount, and supplicated to his Lord, continuing to do so until the setting of the sun.) Allah 3€ loves the throng of people that gather there to supplicate; in fact, He 3 displays them to the angels. The Day of ‘Arafah

Related by At-Tirmidhee (3505) and Ahmad (1465). The commentators of

Al-Musnad said, “Its chain is Hasan.” (Musnad Ahmad (3/66).

Related by Ash-Shaafi’ee in Al-Umm (2/223-224). Al-Albaanee said, “Its chain is weak...but the hadith has other narrations that attest to it. Even if its individual words are weak, when other [chains] are combined with this Mursal [narration] it becomes stronger and rises to the level of Hasan, InshaAllah.” (Refer to As-Silsilatus-Saheehah 1469).

Refer to sahih Muslim (1218).

is a day wherein many people are freed from the Hellfire. ‘Aaisha %, the Mother of the Believers, related (her father #& related the same) that the Messenger of Allah #% said,

15 BS 9B Se JON Se he 8 fT be Bi ge be ws

ONES STG 535 Bri te oid: 243 “On no day does Allah 3€ free more slaves from the Hellfire than on the Day of “Arafah. He 38 indeed draws near, displays them

(the pilgrims) to the angels, and then says, ‘What do these want?’4]

b) When one is at As-Safa and Al-Marwa: When the Prophet 2% came near As-Safa during the Farewell Pilgrimage, he # recited,

Sal wal 33 “Verily! As-Safa and Al-Marwah (two mountains in Makkah) are of the Symbols of Allah’,

after which he # said,

ty er) ix & fash

“T begin with what Allah began with,”

and so he #% began with As-Safa, climbing it until he saw the House (the Ka‘bah). He # then faced the Qiblah, mentioned the phrase of tawhid, and magnified Allah 3%. And he # said,

“None has the right to be worshipped except Allah alone. He fulfilled His promise, aided His servant, and single-handedly defeated the allies.”

He # said the same three times; and during the interval between each time, he # supplicated. Next, he # descended and proceeded to Al-Marwah; he #¢ did the same at Al-Marwah as

' Related by Muslim (1348), An-Nassaaee (3003), and Ibn Maajah (3014).

he # did at As-Safa.""!

During Hajj, After A Pilgrim Pelts The Smaller And Middle Jamarah: Saalim Ibn ‘Abdullah related that ‘Abdullah Ibn ‘Umar would pelt the closest Jamarah with seven pebbles, making Takbeer (i.e., saying, ‘Allahuakbar, Allah is the Greatest) after [the throwing of] each pebble. Then he would proceed, going to low ground, where he would stand, face the Qiblah, and remain standing for a long time. While standing, he would supplicate and raise his hands. Then he would pelt the middle Jamarah in the same manner, after which he would head to his left, to low ground, where he would stand for a long time, facing the Qiblah, supplicating, and raising his hands. Then he would pelt the Dhaat Al- ‘Agabah Jamarah from the inside of the valley, and he would not stand afterwards. [Explaining these actions,] he said, “This is what I saw the Messenger of Allah # do.”

This work was completed on Monday night, the 27th of Rabee’ul-Awwal, 1420 years after the blessed Hijrah (Migration) of the Prophet 2%. All praise is to Allah, and it is by His Grace and Kindness [that good deeds are performed].

'] Muslim related this hadith from Jaabir 4 (1218). "1 Related by Bukhaaree (1753), Ahmad (6368), An-Nassaaee (3083), Ibn

Maajah (3032), and Ad-Daarimee (1903). And the above-mentioned wording is from Bukhaaree’s narration of the hadith.
content_id:
"Ibnu Hajr berkata, "Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Daawood (1048); An-Nassaaee (1389); dan Al-Haakim, dengan sanad Hasan dari Abu Salamah, dari Jaabir #, Marfoo'an (yakni, riwayat tersebut bersumber dari Nabi #%). Dan dalam Shahih Abi Daawood, Al-Albaanee menilai hadith tersebut sebagai 'shahih'.

Bab - 22 Adab-Adab Du'a 497

ditentukan secara pasti, maka orang-orang akan bersandar padanya dan meninggalkan du'a pada waktu-waktu lainnya. Dengan pemahaman ini, sungguh mengherankan melihat orang-orang yang ingin mengetahui secara tepat kapan waktu tersebut."

e) Ketika orang yang berpuasa berbuka puasa: Abu Hurairah meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah #% bersabda,

"Tiga [orang] yang du'anya tidak ditolak: orang yang berpuasa hingga ia berbuka puasa..."

f) Du'a orang yang dizalimi, orang yang sedang dalam perjalanan, dan du'a seorang ayah terhadap anaknya: Ibnu 'Abbaas w meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah #% mengucapkan kata-kata berikut kepada Mu'aadh Ibn Jabal 4 ketika mengutusnya ke Yaman:

abl 25 ks Sf tt Vy dy

"Sesungguhnya engkau akan mendatangi suatu kaum dari Ahli Kitab. Ketika engkau tiba di sana, maka ajaklah mereka untuk bersaksi bahwa tidak ada yang berhak disembah selain Allah dan bahwa Muhammad adalah Rasulullah..."

Dan beliau #% melanjutkan dengan bersabda,

"Dan jagalah dirimu dari du'a orang yang dizalimi, karena sesungguhnya tidak ada tabir antara dia dan Allah."" ]

Dan Abu Hurairah # meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah #% bersabda,

") Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidhee, yang berkata, "Hadith ini Hasan'; oleh Ibn Maajah (1752), dan dalam penilaiannya, Al-Albaanee memutuskan bahwa hadith tersebut shahih (1432-1779).

8585) cpgllsch $325 13s GY Suen cigs bob

"Tiga du'a yang dikabulkan dan tidak ada keraguan padanya (yakni, tidak ada keraguan bahwa ketiganya akan dikabulkan): du'a

orang yang dizalimi, du'a orang yang sedang dalam perjalanan, dan du'a seorang ayah terhadap anaknya."

Orang yang menzalimi orang lain hendaknya berhenti dari kezalimannya, karena tidak ada tabir antara Allah dan orang-orang yang ia zalimi; du'a mereka dikabulkan dengan cepat. Orang yang sedang dalam perjalanan hendaknya memanfaatkan waktunya untuk berdzikir kepada Allah 3%, karena du'anya dikabulkan. Dan para orang tua hendaknya berhati-hati untuk tidak berdu'a kepada Allah 3 yang buruk terhadap anak-anak mereka; du'a seorang ayah dikabulkan, dan dalam panasnya kemarahan, ia mungkin mengucapkan kata-kata yang nantinya akan ia sesali.

g) Saat berperang dan ketika adzan dikumandangkan: Sahl Ibn Sa'ad .% meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah #¢ bersabda,

"Dua [du'a] yang tidak ditolak atau jarang ditolak: du'a pada saat adzan [dikumandangkan] dan ketika pertempuran memanas, ketika mereka [pasukan yang berperang] saling bentrok dan bertempur dengan sengit.""1]

Pada saat tertimpa musibah, atau ketika seseorang memiliki kebutuhan tertentu, seseorang dapat berdu'a dengan doa Dheen-Noon: Sa'ad Ibn Abee Waqqaas # meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah #% bersabda,

Boe EH SY re A oy og 5h5 25 Y ob ss B55

' Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Daawood (2540), dan satu riwayat mengandung tambahan

dari Sahl Ibn Sa'ad 4 dari Nabi #: "Dan saat turunnya hujan." Al-

Albaanee berkata, "Dengan pengecualian, 'dan saat turunnya hujan', hadith tersebut shahih. Ad-Daarimee meriwayatkan hal yang sama (1200).

Bab - 22 Adab-Adab Du'a 499

"Inilah du'a Dheen-Noon yang ia panjatkan ketika berada di dalam perut ikan paus: 'Tidak ada yang berhak disembah selain Engkau (ya Allah); Maha Suci Engkau! Sesungguhnya aku termasuk orang-orang yang zalim.' Tidak ada seorang Muslim pun yang berdu'a dengannya mengenai sesuatu hal melainkan Allah mengabulkan [du'anya] untuknya.'"]

Ketika turun hujan: Berikut ini diriwayatkan dalam sebuah hadith: "Carilah [kesempatan untuk berdu'a] ketika pasukan-pasukan bertemu, ketika iqamah untuk shalat dikumandangkan, dan ketika turun hujan."

Situasi-Situasi di Mana Seseorang Dapat Berharap Du'anya Dikabulkan

Malam 'Arafah, bagi mereka yang berada di sana pada saat Mawgif (mereka yang sedang melaksanakan Hajj): Nabi #% mensyariatkan bahwa, pada Hari 'Arafah, para jamaah haji hendaknya menjama' shalat Dhuhr dan 'Asr serta melaksanakannya pada waktu Dhuhr, agar setiap jamaah haji kemudian dapat membebaskan diri untuk berdu'a kepada Allah 3. Selama Hajj dan pada Hari 'Arafah, ketika Nabi #% selesai melaksanakan kedua shalat tersebut, beliau pergi ke Al-Mawgif, di bawah gunung. Di sana beliau berhenti, tetap berada di atas tunggangannya, dan berdu'a kepada Rabbnya, terus melakukannya hingga matahari terbenam.) Allah 3€ mencintai kerumunan orang-orang yang berkumpul di sana untuk berdu'a; bahkan, Dia 3 memperlihatkan mereka kepada para malaikat. Hari 'Arafah

Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidhee (3505) dan Ahmad (1465). Para pensyarah

Al-Musnad berkata, "Sanadnya Hasan." (Musnad Ahmad (3/66).

Diriwayatkan oleh Ash-Shaafi'ee dalam Al-Umm (2/223-224). Al-Albaanee berkata, "Sanadnya lemah...namun hadith ini memiliki riwayat-riwayat lain yang menguatkannya. Meskipun kata-kata individualnya lemah, ketika [sanad-sanad] lain digabungkan dengan riwayat Mursal ini, ia menjadi lebih kuat dan naik ke tingkat Hasan, InshaAllah." (Rujuk As-Silsilatus-Saheehah 1469).

Rujuk Shahih Muslim (1218).

adalah hari di mana banyak orang dibebaskan dari api Neraka. 'Aaisha %, Ummul Mukminin, meriwayatkan (ayahnya #& meriwayatkan hal yang sama) bahwa Rasulullah #% bersabda,

15 BS 9B Se JON Se he 8 fT be Bi ge be ws

ONES STG 535 Bri te oid: 243 "Tidak ada hari di mana Allah 3€ membebaskan lebih banyak hamba dari api Neraka daripada pada Hari 'Arafah. Dia 3€ sungguh mendekat, memperlihatkan mereka

(para jamaah haji) kepada para malaikat, lalu berfirman, 'Apa yang mereka inginkan?'4]

b) Ketika seseorang berada di As-Safa dan Al-Marwa: Ketika Nabi #% mendekati As-Safa saat Haji Wada', beliau # membaca,

Sal wal 33 "Sesungguhnya As-Safa dan Al-Marwah (dua bukit di Makkah) termasuk Syiar-Syiar Allah',

setelah itu beliau # bersabda,

ty er) ix & fash

"Aku memulai dengan apa yang Allah mulai dengannya,"

sehingga beliau #% memulai dengan As-Safa, mendakinya hingga beliau melihat Baitullah (Ka'bah). Beliau # kemudian menghadap Qiblah, mengucapkan kalimat tawhid, dan mengagungkan Allah 3%. Dan beliau # bersabda,

"Tidak ada yang berhak disembah selain Allah semata. Dia telah memenuhi janji-Nya, menolong hamba-Nya, dan seorang diri mengalahkan sekutu-sekutu."

Beliau # mengucapkan hal yang sama tiga kali; dan di sela-sela setiap kali, beliau # berdu'a. Kemudian, beliau # turun dan berjalan menuju Al-Marwah; beliau #¢ melakukan hal yang sama di Al-Marwah seperti

' Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim (1348), An-Nassaaee (3003), dan Ibn Maajah (3014).

yang beliau # lakukan di As-Safa.""!

Selama Hajj, Setelah Seorang Jamaah Haji Melempar Jamarah Kecil dan Tengah: Saalim Ibn 'Abdullah meriwayatkan bahwa 'Abdullah Ibn 'Umar melempar Jamarah yang paling dekat dengan tujuh kerikil, bertakbeer (yakni, mengucapkan, 'Allahu Akbar, Allah Maha Besar) setelah [lemparan] setiap kerikil. Kemudian beliau melangkah maju, menuju tanah yang rendah, di mana beliau berdiri, menghadap Qiblah, dan berdiri dalam waktu yang lama. Saat berdiri, beliau berdu'a dan mengangkat tangannya. Kemudian beliau melempar Jamarah tengah dengan cara yang sama, setelah itu beliau menuju ke sisi kirinya, ke tanah yang rendah, di mana beliau berdiri dalam waktu yang lama, menghadap Qiblah, berdu'a, dan mengangkat tangannya. Kemudian beliau melempar Jamarah Dhaat Al-'Agabah dari dalam lembah, dan beliau tidak berdiri sesudahnya. [Menjelaskan tindakan-tindakan ini,] beliau berkata, "Inilah yang aku lihat Rasulullah # lakukan."

Karya ini diselesaikan pada malam Senin, tanggal 27 Rabee'ul-Awwal, 1420 tahun setelah Hijrah yang penuh berkah dari Nabi #%. Segala puji bagi Allah, dan hanya dengan Karunia dan Kebaikan-Nya [amal-amal kebaikan dapat dilakukan].

'] Muslim meriwayatkan hadith ini dari Jaabir 4 (1218). "1 Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (1753), Ahmad (6368), An-Nassaaee (3083), Ibn

Maajah (3032), dan Ad-Daarimee (1903). Dan redaksi di atas berasal dari riwayat Bukhaaree tentang hadith tersebut.
content_ar: "I Ton Hajr said, “Related by Abu Daawood (1048); An-Nassaaee (1389); and Al-Haakim, with a حسن chain from Abu Salamah, from Jaabir #, Marfoo’an (i.e., the narration is from النبي ﷺ 3%). And in صحيح Abee Daawood, Al-Albaanee gave the الحديث a ‘صحيح’ grade. الباب - 22 The الآداب Of الدعاء 497 were specified, then people would rely on it and forsake supplicating at other times. With this الفهم in العقل, it is surprising to see people who want لمعرفة precisely عندما the hour is.” e) عندما a الصيام person breaks his fast: أبو هريرة related that الرسول ﷺ of الله #% said, “Three [people] do not have their supplications rejected: the الصيام person حتى he breaks his fast..." f) The الدعاء of a person who is wronged, of the traveler, and of a الأب against his son: Ibn ‘Abbaas w reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله #% said the following words to Mu’aadh Ibn Jabal 4 upon sending him to Yemen: abl 25 ks Sf tt Vy dy “Verily, you are going to a people from the People of the الكتاب. عندما you reach them, then invite them to bear witness that none has the الحق to be worshipped but الله and that محمد ﷺ is الرسول ﷺ of الله...” And he #% went on to say, “And protect yourself from the الدعاء of the wronged person, for indeed, there is no veil between him and الله.”” ] And أبو هريرة # related that الرسول ﷺ of الله 3% said, ") Related by At-Tirmidhee, who said, “This الحديث is حسن’; by Ibn Maajah (1752), and in grading his narration, Al-Albaanee ruled that صح (1432-1779). 8585) cpgllsch $325 13s GY Suen cigs bob “Three supplications that are answered and about which لا شك (i.e., no doubt that they will be answered): the الدعاء of the person who is wronged, the الدعاء of the traveler, and the الدعاء of the الأب against his son.” The person who wrongs others should desist from his الإساءة, for there is no veil between الله and the people he wronged; their supplications are quickly answered. The traveler should take advantage of his time by invoking الله 3%, for his supplications are answered. And الوالدان should be careful not invoke الله 3 against their الأولاد; a الأب’s الدعاء is answered, and in the heat of his الغضب, he may say words that he will later regret. g) خلال battle and عندما the call to الصلاة is made: Sahl Ibn Sa‘ad .% reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله #¢ said, “Two [supplications] are not rejected or are rarely rejected: الدعاء at the time of the call [to الصلاة] and عندما the battle becomes intense, عندما they [the fighting armies] clash and fight intensely.”"1] خلال times of affliction, or عندما one has a specific need, one may supplicate with the invocation of Dheen-Noon: Sa‘ad Ibn Abee Waqqaas # related that الرسول ﷺ of الله 38 said, Boe EH SY re A oy og 5h5 25 Y ob ss B55 ' Related by Abu Daawood (2540), and one narration contains an addition from Sahl Ibn Sa’ad 4 from النبي ﷺ #: “And the time of rain.” Al- Albaanee said, “With the exception of, ‘and the time of rain صح. Ad-Daarimee related the same (1200). الباب - 22 The الآداب Of الدعاء 499 “This is the invocation of Dheen-Noon which he supplicated with عندما he was inside the belly of the whale: ‘None has the الحق to be worshipped but You (O الله); how perfect You are! Verily, I was from the wrongdoers.’ No مسلم man supplicates with it فيما يتعلق بـ anything except that الله answers [his الدعاء] for him.’"] عندما it is raining: The following is related in a الحديث: “Seek to supplicate عندما armies meet, عندما the Iqaamah for الصلاة is made, and عندما it is raining.” Situations Wherein One Can Reasonably الأمل For His Supplications To Be Answered The night of ‘Arafah, for those that are there at the Mawgif (those that are performing الحج): النبي ﷺ 2 legislated that, on the Day of ‘Arafah, pilgrims should combine the Dhuhr and ‘Asr الصلاة and perform them خلال the time for Dhuhr, لذا that each pilgrim can then free himself to supplicate to الله 3. خلال الحج and on the Day of ‘Arafah, عندما النبي ﷺ #% finished performing the two الصلوات, he went to Al-Mawgif, beneath the mountain. There he stopped, remained on his mount, and supplicated to his Lord, continuing to do لذا حتى the setting of the sun.) الله 3€ loves the throng of people that gather there to supplicate; in fact, He 3 displays them to the الملائكة. The Day of ‘Arafah Related by At-Tirmidhee (3505) and أحمد (1465). The commentators of Al-Musnad said, “Its chain is حسن.” (Musnad أحمد (3/66). Related by Ash-Shaafi’ee in Al-Umm (2/223-224). Al-Albaanee said, “Its chain is weak...but the الحديث has other narrations that attest to it. Even إذا its individual words are weak, عندما other [chains] are combined with this Mursal [narration] it becomes stronger and rises to the level of حسن, InshaAllah.” (Refer to As-Silsilatus-Saheehah 1469). Refer to صحيح مسلم (1218). is a day wherein many people are freed from the النار. ‘Aaisha %, the الأم of the المؤمنون, related (her الأب #& related the same) that الرسول ﷺ of الله #% said, 15 BS 9B Se JON Se he 8 fT be Bi ge be ws ONES STG 535 Bri te oid: 243 “On no day does الله 3€ free more slaves from the النار than on the Day of “Arafah. He 38 indeed draws near, displays them (the pilgrims) to the الملائكة, and then says, ‘What do these want?’4] b) عندما one is at As-Safa and Al-Marwa: عندما النبي ﷺ 2% came near As-Safa خلال the Farewell Pilgrimage, he # recited, Sal wal 33 “Verily! As-Safa and Al-Marwah (two mountains in مكة المكرمة) are of the Symbols of الله’, بعد which he # said, ty er) ix & fash “T begin with what الله began with,” and لذا he #% began with As-Safa, climbing it حتى he saw the House (the Ka‘bah). He # then faced the القبلة, mentioned the phrase of tawhid, and magnified الله 3%. And he # said, “None has the الحق to be worshipped except الله alone. He fulfilled His promise, aided His servant, and single-handedly defeated the allies.” He # said the same three times; and خلال the interval between each time, he # supplicated. Next, he # descended and proceeded to Al-Marwah; he #¢ did the same at Al-Marwah as ' Related by مسلم (1348), An-Nassaaee (3003), and Ibn Maajah (3014). he # did at As-Safa.""! خلال الحج, بعد A Pilgrim Pelts The Smaller And Middle Jamarah: Saalim Ibn ‘Abdullah related that ‘Abdullah Ibn ‘عمر would pelt the closest Jamarah with seven pebbles, making Takbeer (i.e., saying, ‘Allahuakbar, الله is the Greatest) بعد [the throwing of] each pebble. Then he would proceed, going to low ground, where he would stand, face the القبلة, and remain standing for a long time. بينما standing, he would supplicate and raise his hands. Then he would pelt the middle Jamarah in the same manner, بعد which he would head to his left, to low ground, where he would stand for a long time, facing the القبلة, supplicating, and raising his hands. Then he would pelt the Dhaat Al- ‘Agabah Jamarah from the inside of the valley, and he would not stand afterwards. [Explaining these actions,] قال, “This is what I saw الرسول ﷺ of الله # do.” This work was completed on Monday night, the 27th of Rabee’ul-Awwal, 1420 years بعد the blessed Hijrah (Migration) of النبي ﷺ 2%. All praise is to الله, and it is by His الفضل and اللطف [that الخير deeds are performed]. '] مسلم related this الحديث from Jaabir 4 (1218). "1 Related by Bukhaaree (1753), أحمد (6368), An-Nassaaee (3083), Ibn Maajah (3032), and Ad-Daarimee (1903). And the above-mentioned wording is from Bukhaaree’s narration of the الحديث.
